stimulus: Residents of a neighborhood in Portland recently founded a community tool library, where neighbors share gardening tools, hand tools, and small appliances. Students at a local high school surveyed users as part of a project to understand the library’s impact. The students concluded that the new tool library benefits the community overall by encouraging neighbors to talk and exchange skills.
stem: Which quotation from a survey respondent would best illustrate the students’ conclusion?

answerOptions.content: “Our first task was figuring out which tools we needed most. We had to balance demand for tools with how much space we had on the shelves.”
“I love being able to borrow a drill without having to drive all the way to a hardware store across town.”
“What I appreciate most is the savings — tools are expensive, and being able to borrow them keeps money in my pocket.”
“I’ve learned so much from my neighbors at the tool library. When I borrowed a tile cutter, one of them walked me through how to use it, and now I’m teaching my own brother how to use it too.”

rationale:
Choice D is correct because the response directly describes neighbors talking and exchanging skills (a neighbor teaches the respondent, who then teaches a relative), which matches the students’ conclusion.

Choice A is incorrect: it describes the planning of the library, not interactions among neighbors. Choice B is incorrect: it focuses on convenience, not skill-sharing. Choice C is incorrect: it focuses on money saved, not on talking and exchanging skills.
